zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Arderne於1305年出生在英國爾阿爾德福德時，她的父親Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I39歲，母親Margaret ferch Gruffudd45歲。她於1330年6月1日與Sir John Wettenhall III在英國爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1385年6月1日在英國英格蘭爾享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de Arderne于1305年出生在英国爾阿爾德福德时，她的父亲Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I39岁，母亲Margaret ferch Gruffudd45岁。她于1330年6月1日与Sir John Wettenhall III在英国爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1385年6月1日在英国英格兰爾享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Arderne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Ardern
meanings: English: locative name variant of Arden . Arden in Cheshire is recorded as Arderne in 1260, and Arden in Warwicks is recorded as Arderne in 1200.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Arderne was born in 1305, in Aldford, Cheshire, England, her father, Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I, was 39 and her mother, Margaret ferch Gruffudd, was 45. She married Sir John Wettenhall III on 1 June 1330, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 1 June 1385, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Arderne est née en 1305, à Aldford, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Margaret ferch Gruffudd, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Wettenhall III le 1 juin 1330, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1385, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Arderne 1305 in Aldford, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I 39 und ihre Mutter Margaret ferch Gruffudd 45. Sie hat am 1. Juni 1330 in Cheshire, England Sir John Wettenhall III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Juni 1385 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Arderne è nata il nel 1305 nel Aldford, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Margaret ferch Gruffudd, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Wettenhall III il 1 giugno 1330, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 1 giugno 1385, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Arderneが1305年にイギリスチェシュアイアアルドフォードで生まれた時，父Sir Knt. John d'Arderne Iは39歳，母Margaret ferch Gruffuddは45歳であった。1330年6月1日にイギリスチェシュアイアでSir John Wettenhall IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1385年6月1日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアで80歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Arderne(이)가 1305년 영국 체실르 알드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret ferch Gruffudd(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1330년 6월 1일 영국 체실르에서 Sir John Wettenhall III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1385년 6월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Arderne nasceu em 1305, em Aldford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Margaret ferch Gruffudd, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Wettenhall III em 1 de junho de 1330, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de junho de 1385, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Arderne родилась в 1305 г. в местности алдфорд, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret ferch Gruffudd, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Wettenhall III 1 июня 1330 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 1 июня 1385 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de Arderne nació en 1305, en Aldford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Knt. John d'Arderne I, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Margaret ferch Gruffudd, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Sir John Wettenhall III el 1 de junio de 1330, en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 1 de junio de 1385, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 80 años.
